programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Shibuya is one of Japan's most famous tourist spots, but the low-lying city is also at risk of flooding and earthquakes - we look at Shibuya's advanced efforts to prepare for disasters.
Shiga / Nagayama Yuko (architect). For 400 years, a group of stonemasons have been building walls using natural stone. Nagayama learns how a stone's qualities are assessed and utilised.
Kataoka Ainosuke takes his first turn as our host. Ainosuke is a renowned kabuki actor. He'll be our guide to the attractions of kabuki looking at some of the roles he's played.
This flagship documentary series offers must-see episodes on politics, the economy, social trends, science and the environment in Japan and beyond.

Japan's exciting music scene with pop, rock, traditional, live performances, artist interviews, all the latest news, video releases and more!
An animated series about a Japanese dog who gets lost in New York City. Charo says 'I want to see my owner again!', and so he starts his adventure to find his way back home. (S1, E01)
Frozen food products widely available in Japan include hundreds of pasta dishes and even sushi - new flash-freezing techniques are improving quality, reducing food waste, and driving innovation.

programmes.show_description: Japan has a long history of natural disasters - Bosai explores how to overcome them and save lives with the power of science.
We look at the unique world of the Japanese language accompanied with scenes from Japan - many fascinating aspects of Japanese like onomatopoeia are also introduced.
#15 To the monkey hot spring, please. / To tell a taxi driver where you want to go, just say the destination and 'made onegai-shimasu'.
